zh.title: Solana/美元技术分析：高位震荡偏多整理结构
zh.content:
Solana/美元当前呈现高位震荡偏强结构，价格稳于71上方，短线多头仍占主导，但上行动能有所放缓，进入整理阶段，整体偏多运行中。  

MACD指标维持在零轴上方，动能柱略有扩张，显示多头动能仍在延续；价格站稳SMA20上方，短期趋势保持向上但强度一般，略显震荡。  

上方阻力集中在71.40附近，突破后有望测试72关口；下方支撑位于69.20与68.50一带，跌破将削弱短期结构，区间震荡为主。  

建议以回调低吸为主，关注70-69区间支撑有效性，若放量突破71.4可顺势跟进，否则高位谨慎短线博弈，控制风险，操作纪律。

en.title: Solana vs US Dollar Technical Analysis
en.content:
SOL/USD is currently exhibiting a mild bullish consolidation phase, trading around 71.25 after a very narrow 24-hour range between 71.07 and 71.43. Price action remains compressed, suggesting low volatility and equilibrium between buyers and sellers. However, maintaining levels above the 20-period SMA at 69.24 indicates that the broader short-term structure still favors an upward bias, with buyers gradually defending higher lows.

From an indicator perspective, momentum signals lean cautiously bullish. The MACD reading at 0.87 suggests positive momentum is present, although the absence of a visible signal line limits confirmation of a full crossover structure. RSI data is unavailable, which reduces clarity on overbought or oversold conditions. Still, trading above the SMA(20) typically reflects sustained short-term strength and suggests dip-buying interest remains active near support zones.

Key levels remain tightly defined due to the current compression. Immediate resistance is located at the recent high of 71.43, where repeated rejection has already formed. A clean breakout above this level could open a path toward 72.50 and potentially 73.00. On the downside, initial support sits at 71.07, with stronger structural support at the SMA(20) near 69.24, which would be critical if volatility expands lower.

Trading strategy favors range discipline with breakout readiness. Short-term traders may consider buying near 71.10–71.20 with tight risk control, or alternatively wait for a confirmed breakout above 71.43 to join momentum continuation. Failure to hold 71.07 would weaken the structure and shift focus back toward the 69 region. Risk management remains essential given the current low-volatility compression phase.
